channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: US court tells Israeli company to stop targeting WhatsApp
item.description: WASHINGTON, Oct 18 (Reuters) - A U.S. court has ordered Israel's NSO Group to stop targeting Meta Platforms WhatsApp messaging service, a development the spyware company warned could put it out of business. In a 25-page ruling handed down Friday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Raphael Satter, NewsNation

item.title: Tesla's self-driving cars under fire again
item.description: Tesla's Full Self-Driving system faces federal investigation following 58 reports of crashes, with six vehicles running red lights before colliding with other cars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Toonstar’s new ‘Uncle Roger’ cartoon embraces AI—but slop it’s not
item.description: “The gyoza needs to look a little whiter. It’s too pink.” Nigel Ng is genially micromanaging the look and feel of Fried, an animated series that will premiere on YouTube later this year.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Larry Magid: Google refines its Pixel Watch
item.description: I’ve tested the three previous versions of Google’s watch, and the new one stands out with a new design and some important new features.
item.dc:creator: Larry Magid, The Mercury News

item.title: Australia sharing tips on curbing social media for children before age limit starts in December
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — The Australian government has begun a public education campaign with tips on how to wean children off social media ahead of a world-first national 16-year age limit taking effect in December.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Booker courts red state voters online, urging them to join ‘No Kings’ protests
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) is pressing red state voters to join local “No Kings” protests Saturday and put pressure on their GOP legislators to end the government shutdown through his outreach with social media influencers and creators in Florida and North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, The Hill

item.title: Security experts warn of increased cyber risk after end of Windows 10 support
item.description: Cyber security experts are warning Windows 10 users of an increased risk of malicious activity after support officially ended Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, NewsNation

item.title: Death of kidnapped South Korean student spurs talks with Cambodia to tackle online scams
item.description: PHNOM PENH, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian and South Korean officials agreed Thursday to cooperate in combating online scams, after the death of a South Korean student who was reportedly forced to work in a scam center in Cambodia triggered public outrage in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top Army official using ChatGPT to make military decisions: Report
item.description: An expert warned that, no matter how fast the tech can give an answer, nothing can replace human judgment when it comes to military calls.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Using Valkey on Azure and in .NET Aspire
item.description: Big changes to the license used by the popular open source key/value store Redis prompted a fork, with the launch of Valkey. In the time since that fork in March 2024, the two projects have diverged.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to London in 2026
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Robotaxi pioneer Waymo plans to expand to London next year, marking the company’s latest step in rolling out its driverless ride service internationally.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Lawsuit accuses Amazon of 'fake' Prime Day deals
item.description: Two people are suing Amazon, accusing the retail giant of advertising "fake sales" and misleading consumers during the company's popular "Prime Day" events.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX launches the 11th test flight of its mega Starship rocket with another win
item.description: SpaceX launched another of its mammoth Starship rockets on a test flight Monday, successfully making it halfway around the world while releasing mock satellites like last time. Starship — the biggest and most powerful rocket ever built — thundered into the evening sky from the southern tip of Texas.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenAI partners with Broadcom to design its own AI chips
item.description: OpenAI said Monday it is working with chipmaker Broadcom to design its own artificial intelligence computer chips. The two California companies didn't disclose the financial terms of the deal but said they will start deploying the new racks of customized “AI accelerators” late next year.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Inside Nvidia’s ‘grid-to-chip’ vision: How Vera Rubin and Spectrum-XGS push toward AI giga-factories
item.description: Nvidia will be front-and-center at this week’s Global Summit for members of the Open Compute Project (OCP), emphasizing its “grid-to-chip” philosophy.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Federal, industry IT leaders on how agencies are preparing for post-quantum cybersecurity
item.description: Air Force, CISA, NIST share how they are preparing for a future when quantum computers could break today’s encryption. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Federal, industry IT leaders on how agencies are preparing for post-quantum cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: How to run RAG projects for better data analytics results
item.description: The arrival of generative AI-enhanced business intelligence (GenBI) for enterprise data analytics has opened up access to insights, while also increasing the speed, relevance and accuracy of those insights. But that’s in best-case scenarios.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Ethernet vs Wi-Fi security comparison reveals surprising results for home users seeking protection
item.description: Expert analysis reveals whether wired Ethernet or wireless Wi-Fi connections are safer for home internet use, plus practical steps to secure your network from attackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
